financial_performance: Total net sales increased 6% in 2025 compared to 2024.
Americas net sales increased during 2025 primarily due to higher iPhone and Services sales.
Europe net sales increased during 2025 primarily due to higher Services, iPhone and Mac sales.
Greater China net sales decreased during 2025 primarily due to lower iPhone sales.
Japan net sales increased during 2025 primarily due to higher iPhone, Services and iPad sales.
Rest of Asia Pacific net sales increased during 2025 primarily due to higher iPhone, Services and Mac sales.
iPhone net sales increased during 2025 due to higher Pro models sales.
Mac net sales increased during 2025 primarily due to higher laptop and desktop sales.
iPad net sales increased during 2025 primarily due to higher iPad Air, iPad mini and iPad sales.
Wearables, Home and Accessories net sales decreased during 2025 primarily due to lower Accessories and Wearables sales.
Services net sales increased during 2025 primarily due to higher advertising, App Store and cloud services sales.
Products gross margin increased during 2025 primarily due to favorable costs and different product mix.
Products gross margin percentage decreased during 2025 due to different product mix and tariff costs.
Services gross margin increased during 2025 due to higher Services net sales and different service mix.
Services gross margin percentage increased during 2025 primarily due to different service mix.
R&D expense growth during 2025 was primarily driven by increases in headcount-related expenses and infrastructure costs.
Selling, general and administrative expense growth during 2025 was primarily driven by headcount-related and variable selling expenses.
Effective tax rate for 2025 was lower than 2024 due to $10.7 billion decrease in State Aid Decision provision.
Company's net sales and gross margins are subject to volatility and downward pressure due to various factors.
Gross margins vary significantly across products, services, geographic segments and distribution channels and can change over time.
Company generates a significant portion of net sales from a single product category.
Weakening of foreign currencies relative to U.S. dollar adversely affects foreign currency-denominated sales and earnings.
Strengthening of foreign currencies may increase cost of product components denominated in those currencies.
guidance_and_forward_outlook: Company intends to increase its dividend on an annual basis, subject to Board declaration.
Company believes gross margins will be subject to volatility and downward pressure.
Company believes cash balances and operations will satisfy cash requirements and capital return program over next 12 months.
Company expects to make further business changes in the future.
Company has invested and will continue to invest in programs to enhance reseller sales.
Company may invest in new business strategies, commercial relationships and acquisitions in the future.
Future dividends are subject to declaration by the Company's Board of Directors.
Company's share repurchase program does not obligate it to acquire any specific number of shares.
material_events: New U.S. Tariffs were announced beginning in second quarter 2025 on imports from multiple countries.
Additional tariffs announced on imports from China, India, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, Vietnam and the European Union.
Several countries have imposed, or threatened to impose, reciprocal tariffs on imports from the U.S.
U.S. Department of Commerce initiated investigation under Section 232 into semiconductor imports and derivative products.
Company announced new $100 billion share repurchase program in May 2025.
Company raised quarterly dividend from $0.25 to $0.26 per share beginning May 2025.
Company repurchased $89.3 billion of common stock during 2025.
Company paid dividends and dividend equivalents of $15.4 billion during 2025.
Google was found to have violated U.S. antitrust laws on August 5, 2024.
U.S. District Court for District of Columbia ordered certain remedies on September 2, 2025.
Company implemented changes to iOS, iPadOS, App Store and Safari in EU to comply with Digital Markets Act.
Company implemented changes to how developers communicate with consumers within apps on U.S. storefront.
Company is currently subject to court order preventing commission or fee on certain consumer purchases.
risk_factors_emphasized: Company's operations depend significantly on global and regional economic conditions.
Sales outside the U.S. represent a majority of the Company's total net sales.
Majority of supplier facilities, including manufacturing and assembly sites, are located outside the U.S.
Restrictions on international trade can materially adversely affect the Company's business and supply chain.
Company has minority market share in global smartphone, personal computer, tablet and wearables markets.
Significant majority of manufacturing performed by outsourcing partners located primarily in China mainland, India, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan and Vietnam.
Company obtains certain components from single or limited sources, subject to significant supply and pricing risks.
Global semiconductor industry has experienced high demand and shortages of supply in the past.
Company remains subject to significant risks of supply shortages and price increases.
Introduction of new and complex technologies, such as artificial intelligence features, can increase safety risks.
Company is at relatively greater risk of being targeted for malicious attacks because of high profile.
Risks may be exacerbated as new and emerging technologies are further integrated into products and services.
Macroeconomic conditions including inflation, interest rates and currency fluctuations could materially impact results and financial condition.
Tariffs and other measures applied to Company's products or components can have material adverse impact.
Trade and international disputes can adversely impact macroeconomic environment and consumer spending.
Ultimate tariff impact remains uncertain and depends on additional measures and retaliatory responses.
Company's future gross margins can be impacted by variety of factors discussed in Risk Factors.
explicit_uncertainties: Company may not be able to accurately predict, control or mitigate these risks.
Ultimate impact remains uncertain and will depend on several factors.
Company may not be able to effectively mitigate any or all adverse impacts from such measures.
Various modifications to the U.S. Tariffs have been announced and further changes could be made in the future.
Company may not be able to develop and market new products and services successfully.
Company may not be able to successfully manage future introductions and transitions of products and services.
Company may not be able to detect and fix all issues and defects in hardware, software and services.
Company is not always able to obtain all necessary licenses to third-party intellectual property rights.
Company may be unable to continue to offer wide variety of content at commercially reasonable prices.
Outcome of litigation or government investigations is inherently uncertain.
New business strategies and ventures are inherently risky and may not be successful.
Ultimate tariff impact remains uncertain and will depend on several factors including additional measures.
Outcomes of legal proceedings and claims are inherently uncertain.
No assurance can be given that final outcome of tax uncertainties will match reserves.
Resolution of uncertainties inconsistent with expectations could have material impact on financial condition and results.
notable_causal_statements: Americas net sales increased primarily due to higher net sales of iPhone and Services.
Europe net sales increased primarily due to higher net sales of Services, iPhone and Mac.
Greater China net sales decreased primarily due to lower net sales of iPhone.
Japan net sales increased primarily due to higher net sales of iPhone, Services and iPad.
iPhone net sales increased due to higher net sales of Pro models.
Mac net sales increased primarily due to higher net sales of laptops and desktops.
Products gross margin increased primarily due to favorable costs and different mix of products.
Products gross margin percentage decreased primarily due to different mix of products and tariff costs.
Services gross margin increased primarily due to higher Services net sales and different mix of services.
R&D expense growth was primarily driven by increases in headcount-related expenses and infrastructure-related costs.
Effective tax rate for 2025 was lower due to $10.7 billion decrease in State Aid Decision provision.
Adverse macroeconomic conditions can adversely impact consumer confidence and spending and materially adversely affect demand for products and services.
Restrictive measures can increase the cost or limit the availability of the Company's products and components.
Trade disputes can result in shifts and reductions in consumer spending and negative consumer sentiment.
Such events can make it difficult or impossible to manufacture and deliver products to customers.
Because Company relies on single or limited sources, business interruption would exacerbate negative consequences.
Quality problems can adversely affect user experience and result in harm to Company's reputation.
Component defects could make Company's products unsafe and create risk of environmental or property damage.
Failure to obtain right to use third-party intellectual property can require Company to modify certain products.
If third-party software applications cease to be developed for Company's products, customers may choose not to buy.
Changes to Company's business in response to DMA could materially adversely affect business and results.
